title: pouteria obovata
intro: Pouteria obovata, also known as the yellow sapote, is a tropical evergreen tree native to Central America and southern Mexico, characterized by its yellow or orange fruit and edible pulp. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. The health benefits of Pouteria obovata include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral agent, making it effective in treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Pouteria obovata can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat issues such as diarrhea, coughs, and respiratory problems.
